hypothesis: If the MatAgent framework's knowledge base were dynamically updated in real-time by incorporating LLM-generated explanations of newly proposed composition-property transitions discovered during each iterative run—rather than relying on a static pre-constructed database of 10,000 random pairs—then the framework would exhibit significantly accelerated convergence toward challenging target properties (e.g., −3.8 eV/atom) and higher V.U.N. scores, because the self-expanding knowledge base would progressively encode task-specific composition-property relationships that are systematically more relevant to the current exploration trajectory than relationships sampled uniformly at random from the Materials Project.
